payload.description: Implement GitHub issue 807 on top of the SQLite projection cutover from PR 949. Add hidden pending summary preparation, pending condensation, atomic publish into canonical summaries/context items, and async LLM preparation that does not block foreground turns. Required base: lossless-claw PR 949 head db7f7ddaecd82271ae57638e514bbedb20ca41f6 or successor with the same SQLite projection semantics; OpenClaw PR 96625 head 71f101dd16fa28ce8212e6e683dc897823b3a55b or successor with the required runtime capabilities.
payload.title: Implement background pending summary compaction

payload.description: Add migration tables and typed store methods for pending summary batches, pending summary nodes, node source coverage, node child edges, claim leases, status transitions, and promoted canonical summary ids. Include focused DB/store tests that prove pending rows are not canonical summaries.
payload.title: Add pending summary schema and store

payload.description: Build pure planning logic for compactable-prefix snapshots, leaf chunk planning, condensation-layer planning, and publish-frontier selection. Keep pending nodes hidden until publish. Add unit tests for coverage, gaps, stale boundary handling, and mixed-depth frontier selection.
payload.title: Add pending summary planner

payload.description: Add background worker plumbing that claims one pending node, releases DB/session locks before LLM calls, writes pending results in a short transaction, and handles retry/stale/failure status. Add tests proving LLM execution is outside the store transaction or session queue.
payload.title: Add async pending preparation worker

payload.description: Canonicalize a ready pending DAG frontier in one DB transaction: insert canonical summaries, source message links, parent links, swap all covered context item ranges to frontier summaries, resequence context items, and mark pending rows promoted. Add stale fingerprint, fresh-tail, retry, and crash-safety tests.
payload.title: Add atomic pending publish

payload.description: Wire pending planning, preparation, and publish into the SQLite-backed context engine lifecycle on PR 949. Ensure assemble/search/doctor/focus readers ignore pending state and foreground turns do not wait on background LLM preparation.
payload.title: Integrate issue 807 lifecycle

payload.description: Update user-facing docs/config references, add changeset if behavior is release-visible, run focused and broad validation, and capture any reusable implementation learning in Martian Memory.
payload.title: Document and validate pending compaction

payload.description: Background deferred compaction drains should not immediately reschedule while a maintenance row is still in nextAttemptAfter backoff; current 952 logs show tight repeated backoff/done lines for conversation 4849.
payload.title: Fix deferred compaction backoff drain loop

payload.description: The provider-fallback maintenance test still expects legacy compacted-but-over-target retry debt, but PR 952 pending summaries can fully publish within the step cap and should clear maintenance instead.
payload.title: Align maintenance test with pending publish result

payload.description: Lossless plugin dependency model resolution preferred the configured primary summaryModel over explicit fallback provider modelRef candidates. This collapsed configured fallbackProviders onto the primary model, so summary timeouts went straight to deterministic fallback instead of trying fallback models.
payload.title: Fix Lossless fallback provider model resolution

payload.description: Background pending-summary maintenance should prepare ready summaries without publishing them. Manual compact, threshold inline compact, and emergency over-budget assembly should publish ready pending summaries.
payload.title: Defer pending summary publish until compaction is needed

payload.description: Adjust the pending summary compaction proof harness to assert prepare-only ready state before explicit publish.
payload.title: Update pending summary E2E proof for deferred publish

payload.description:
Follow-up epic addressing defects found in review of the lc-8cb pending-summary-compaction branch and confirmed against the live lcm.db (conversation 4854, agent:main:main).

Two evidence sources:
1. Test isolation: full suite has 10 failures on this branch; running the failing files at pre-epic commit 82c1524 with identical node_modules shows 3 are pre-existing (log-redaction, environmental) and 7 are introduced by this epic (4x lcm-command rollover doctor, 2x circuit-breaker reason strings, 1x engine-fidelity reason string).
2. Live DB: conversation 4854 shows a 15-minute heartbeat loop where extendReadyBatchIfRangeGrew() creates one ~70-token leaf AND one whole-prefix ~8-9KB condensed re-summarization per cycle (condensed nodes 2-326, 2-328, 2-330 ... 2-340, all 'ready', all but the newest dead weight). Unbounded LLM spend on an idle session; ~96 redundant full-prefix summarize calls/day.

Subtasks are ordered by priority: test regressions first (merge blockers), then the spend-loop fixes, then resilience/hygiene.
payload.title: Harden pending summary compaction (lc-8cb follow-up)

payload.description:
REGRESSION (4 failing tests in test/lcm-command.test.ts): the epic added conversation-scoped tables pending_compaction_batches and pending_summary_nodes (src/db/migration.ts ~1164-1240) but did not register them in HANDLED_CONVERSATION_ID_TABLES in src/plugin/lcm-doctor-rollover-splits.ts. The doctor's safety scan (loadUnhandledConversationIdTables) enumerates tables with a conversation_id column and pushes 'unhandled conversation tables: ...' into every lane's review reasons, so classifySessionKeyGroup() now classifies ALL lanes as needs-review and 'doctor apply rollover-splits' refuses all repairs ('safe lanes: 0', 'skipped for review: 1').

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Add 'pending_compaction_batches' and 'pending_summary_nodes' to HANDLED_CONVERSATION_ID_TABLES.
2. Teach the repair to actually handle them. Semantics: pending rows are disposable prepared work keyed to per-conversation context_items ordinals. A lane merge rewrites conversation_id and resequences context items, which invalidates every ordinal-keyed pending node and every batch source fingerprint. The correct repair is to DELETE pending_compaction_batches rows for ALL conversations in the lane (sources AND target) during repair — child pending_summary_nodes / node_messages / node_children cascade via ON DELETE CASCADE. Do NOT try to remap ordinals; the batches are reproducible.
3. Note: pending_summary_node_messages / _children have no conversation_id column so the scanner does not flag them; they are handled transitively by the cascade. pending_summary_node_messages references messages(message_id) ON DELETE RESTRICT — deleting batches BEFORE any message merge avoids FK failures; order the delete first in the repair transaction.
4. Fix should make these 4 tests pass again: 'surfaces rollover split memory from the default status command', 'reports whole-DB rollover split memory even when no current conversation is resolved', 'repairs safe whole-DB rollover splits after confirmation', 'repairs rollover splits when unrelated foreign key issues already exist'.
5. Add one new test: a lane with pending batch rows classifies as safe and repair deletes the pending rows.

VERIFY: npx vitest run test/lcm-command.test.ts
payload.title: Register pending tables with rollover-split doctor

payload.description:
REGRESSION (3 failing tests): routing compact()/maintenance through executePendingCompactionCore (src/engine.ts ~1005-1172) changed the reason strings surfaced to hosts and tests.

FAILURES:
1. test/circuit-breaker.test.ts 'does not reclassify repeated auth failures as summary spend failures': expects reason to contain 'provider auth failure'; gets raw failureSummary '[lcm] compaction failed: provider auth error (401)...'.
2. test/circuit-breaker.test.ts 'should trip after auth failure during later full-sweep passes': expects first.reason === 'provider auth failure after partial compaction'; same raw string received. (Note: second.reason === 'circuit breaker open' still works — recordCompactionAuthFailure IS called in the pending core failed branch at engine.ts ~1095.)
3. test/engine-fidelity.test.ts 'force compaction clears poor-reduction spend backoff...': expects 'could not reach target'; gets 'stored compaction exhausted but live context still exceeds target; transcript reset required'.

IMPLEMENTATION: investigate where the legacy path normalized these strings (executeCompactionCore / compaction.ts) and restore the same normalization in executePendingCompactionCore's failed branch: when lastResult.authFailure is true, surface the legacy-normalized reason ('provider auth failure' / '...after partial compaction' when prior steps prepared/published in this call) while keeping the raw provider detail in the error field. For failure 3, trace why the pending path reaches the 'stored compaction exhausted...' branch where legacy returned 'could not reach target' — likely the legacy-fallback invocation inside the pending core (engine.ts ~1128 runLegacyCompaction) surfaces a different exhaustion reason; the fix may be in how executePendingCompactionCore forwards the fallback result rather than in compaction.ts.

DECISION RULE: prefer restoring the old strings (hosts/tests encode them as contract). Only update a test expectation if the new string is strictly more truthful AND nothing matches on the old one — grep src/ and the openclaw host surface for matches on the old strings before deciding.

VERIFY: npx vitest run test/circuit-breaker.test.ts test/engine-fidelity.test.ts
payload.title: Restore compaction failure-reason contract through pending core

payload.description:
ACTIVE SPEND LEAK (confirmed in live ~/.openclaw/lcm.db, conversation 4854 agent:main:main): every 15-minute heartbeat adds 2 messages (~70-90 tokens); the afterTurn leaf trigger fires (evaluateLeafTrigger: rawTokensOutsideTail >= leafChunkTokens stays true forever because prepared-but-unpublished summaries do not reduce raw tokens); the prepare drain runs; extendReadyBatchIfRangeGrew() (src/pending-summary-coordinator.ts ~247-330) extends for ANY suffix growth, planning a ~70-token leaf node per cycle (and, until the sibling issue is fixed, a whole-prefix condensed rebuild). planPendingLeafNodes treats leafChunkTokens as a maximum only — there is no minimum useful chunk size, contra AN 0002's chunking policy.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. In extendReadyBatchIfRangeGrew, before planning, compute the suffix delta: sum of tokenCount over snapshot.items with itemType === 'message' and ordinal > batch.compactableEndOrdinal and ordinal <= snapshot.compactableEndOrdinal.
2. If delta < config.leafChunkTokens, return null (caller then returns status 'ready', which correctly reports the existing frontier and stops the drain). This mirrors the original leaf-trigger semantics applied to the DELTA instead of the total.
3. Do NOT gate the initial planBatch() path — only extension.
4. Consider making the threshold explicit: reuse leafChunkTokens rather than adding a new config knob (AN treats leafChunkTokens as the minimum useful leaf size).

TESTS: add to test/pending-summary-coordinator.test.ts: (a) heartbeat pattern — ready batch + 2 tiny new messages -> runOnce(prepare-only) returns 'ready' and creates NO new nodes; (b) growth >= leafChunkTokens -> extension plans exactly the new leaf(s). Also confirm existing test 'extends a ready unpublished batch when the compactable prefix grows' still passes (its fixture may need suffix tokens >= leafChunkTokens now — adjust fixture, not the assertion intent).

VERIFY: npx vitest run test/pending-summary-coordinator.test.ts
payload.title: Gate pending batch extension on meaningful suffix growth

payload.description:
ACTIVE SPEND LEAK, part 2 (live DB, conversation 4854): each extension cycle planned a brand-new whole-prefix condensed node (2-326, 2-328, ..., 2-340 — nine depth-1 nodes, each ~2K summary tokens over ~8-9KB of child content, each a full LLM re-summarization of an almost-identical prefix). Verified children of condensed 2-340: the same 9 canonical summaries + ALL 11 leaves. Only the newest node is ever usable by selectPendingPublishFrontier (highest depth, longest range wins); the previous 8 are dead weight.

ROOT CAUSE (two parts):
1. planPendingCondensedNodes (src/pending-summary-planner.ts ~202-291) dedups only EXACT (kind, depth, ordinalStart, ordinalEnd) matches (coordinator plannerNodeIdentityKey, planner's own filter). A superset range differing by one tiny leaf always survives and is planned as a fresh node.
2. Token-count asymmetry explains why initial planning does NOT build whole-prefix parents but extensions DO: at planBatch time leaves carry RAW source token counts (~chunk budget) so a depth-0 group blows condensedChunkTokens; at extension time pendingRecordToPlannerNode feeds ACTUAL summary token counts (72-456), so the whole prefix now fits in one depth-0 group -> new parent every cycle.

IMPLEMENTATION (preferred approach — makes the DAG grow layered, as AN 0002 intended):
In extendReadyBatchIfRangeGrew (src/pending-summary-coordinator.ts ~264-304), before calling planPendingCondensedNodes, remove from the candidate set any node whose ordinal range is fully covered by an existing READY condensed node of the same batch at a higher depth (i.e., the depth-0 children already condensed into 2-326 should not be re-groupable). Feed the ready condensed node itself as the reusable child (already happens via reusablePendingNodes). Result: the planner can only build [condensed 2-N + new leaves] -> depth-2 parent, and only when that group independently satisfies condensedMinFanout AND condensedMinSourceTokens — so tiny suffixes accumulate as leaves until the fanout/token policy is genuinely met.
ALTERNATIVE (if the preferred approach fights the layer logic): post-filter planned condensed nodes in the coordinator — drop any planned condensed node N when a ready condensed node E exists with same start and E.ordinalEnd < N.ordinalEnd, unless the children of N outside E's coverage satisfy minFanout/minSourceTokens on their own.

TESTS (test/pending-summary-coordinator.test.ts): (a) batch with ready whole-prefix condensed 2-N + one new small leaf -> extension plans NO new condensed node; (b) after enough new leaves accumulate to satisfy fanout+token policy -> exactly one new condensed parent whose children are [ready condensed 2-N, new leaves...] (verify via pending_summary_node_children links, preserving order); (c) frontier selection still finds full coverage in both states.

DEPENDS ON: the extension-gating fix (do it after; its tests will change the same fixtures).
VERIFY: npx vitest run test/pending-summary-coordinator.test.ts test/pending-summary-planner.test.ts
payload.title: Delta-gate condensed planning to stop whole-prefix rebuilds

payload.description:
RESILIENCE / RE-SPEND RISK (deviation from AN 0002, which required 'claim leases, retry, stale-batch detection, failure status'): in PendingCompactionCoordinator.runOnce (src/pending-summary-coordinator.ts ~205-217), ANY single node preparation failure calls markBatchStale on the whole batch — destroying all READY sibling content. claimNextPlannedNode (src/store/pending-summary-store.ts ~644-742) only claims 'planned' or lease-expired 'running' nodes, never 'failed', so without the batch-stale escalation a failed node would deadlock the batch; but with it, one transient LLM 500 at node k forces a full replan (planBatch never reuses ready nodes from stale batches) and re-summarizes nodes 0..k-1 from scratch. Persistent non-auth failure on one chunk = re-spend of the whole prefix on every retriggered cycle, bounded only by the auth-only circuit breaker.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Schema (src/db/migration.ts): add retry_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 to pending_summary_nodes (additive migration; follow the existing migration style used elsewhere in the file for released tables — check whether pending tables have shipped in a release; if not, editing CREATE TABLE in place may be acceptable per repo convention — verify how lc-8cb handled schema revisions).
2. Store: markNodeFailed increments retry_count. claimNextPlannedNode also claims status='failed' nodes WHERE retry_count < 3 (constant, or config), with a backoff window: updated_at <= now - min(60s * 2^retry_count, 15m). Keep lease semantics identical.
3. Coordinator: in the prepared.status==='failed' branch, only markBatchStale when (a) the failure is an auth failure (breaker handles global stop) OR (b) the failed node has exhausted retries. Otherwise return the failed result WITHOUT staling — the node stays claimable after backoff.
4. Worker (src/pending-summary-worker.ts) needs no change — markNodeFailed guards by lease.

TESTS (test/pending-summary-store.test.ts + coordinator): (a) failed node with retry_count<3 is reclaimable after backoff and batch survives; (b) node failing 3x -> batch stale; (c) ready siblings retain content until terminal staleness; (d) auth failure still stales immediately + trips breaker. Update existing test 'replans the same projection after a failed pending preparation' to the new semantics (it currently asserts the stale-then-replan behavior for FIRST failure — change it to exercise retry exhaustion).

VERIFY: npx vitest run test/pending-summary-store.test.ts test/pending-summary-coordinator.test.ts test/pending-summary-worker.test.ts
payload.title: Retry failed pending nodes instead of staling the whole batch

payload.description:
HYGIENE (AN 0002: 'The default should mark pending rows promoted and remove heavy pending payloads later' — the 'later' part was never implemented). Live DB shows 8 superseded whole-prefix condensed nodes (~8KB content each) parked as 'ready' forever in one conversation after 2 hours; stale/published batches also keep full content indefinitely. No reader ever uses them: claimNextPlannedNode only claims from batches in planning/ready/publishing status, frontier selection only uses the newest covering node.

IMPLEMENTATION — add a cleanup pass to PendingSummaryStore + call it from the coordinator:
1. Store method pruneSupersededNodes(batchId): delete READY (not promoted) condensed nodes N of a batch when another READY/PROMOTED node exists in the same batch with same depth, same ordinalStart, and strictly greater ordinalEnd, AND N is not referenced as child_node_id in pending_summary_node_children by any non-stale node. (The 2-326..2-338 chain in conv 4854 satisfies this; each superseded node is unreferenced because new parents link leaves directly.)
2. Store method clearPromotedPayloads(batchId): after publish, SET content = NULL on promoted nodes — canonical_summary_id + metadata remain for doctor/crash-recovery lineage per the AN. IMPORTANT: only safe because loadSourceText reads pending content solely for condensed preparation of ready (unpublished) children; after batch publish nothing prepares from it. Do NOT clear content on ready nodes of active batches.
3. Store method deleteFinishedBatches(olderThan): delete batches with status IN ('stale','failed','published') and updated_at older than a retention window (suggest 7 days; batches cascade to nodes/messages/children). Note pending_summary_node_messages FK to messages is ON DELETE RESTRICT in the OTHER direction — deleting pending rows is unrestricted.
4. Wire-up: call pruneSupersededNodes after successful extension planning (coordinator extendReadyBatchIfRangeGrew), clearPromotedPayloads inside/after publishReadyFrontier success (publisher or coordinator), deleteFinishedBatches opportunistically in runOnce when it observes a finished batch (cheap indexed query; no scheduler needed).

TESTS (test/pending-summary-store.test.ts): superseded unreferenced ready node deleted; referenced child never deleted; promoted payload cleared but canonical_summary_id kept; old stale batch fully removed; active batch untouched.

VERIFY: npx vitest run test/pending-summary-store.test.ts test/pending-summary-coordinator.test.ts test/pending-summary-publisher.test.ts
payload.title: GC superseded and stale pending summary rows

payload.description:
GUARD GAP: executePendingCompactionCore (src/engine.ts ~1005-1070) checks only the auth circuit breaker (compactionGuards.isCircuitBreakerOpen) before running preparation LLM calls. The legacy compaction path also honors the summary-spend scope backoff (compactionGuards.getSummarySpendBackoffUntil / poor-reduction spend guard, see its use in consumeDeferredCompactionDebt at engine.ts ~841-844, ~965-967). Background prepare-only drains therefore bypass the runaway-spend protections entirely — and the conversation-4854 incident is exactly the runaway-spend shape those guards exist for.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Read src/compaction-guards.ts to get the exact API surface (resolveSummarySpendScope, getSummarySpendBackoffUntil, and whatever records spend — find how executeCompactionCore records summary spend after LLM calls).
2. In executePendingCompactionCore, before the runOnce loop: resolve the spend scope key (same as consumeDeferredCompactionDebt does: resolveSummarySpendScope({kind:'compaction', scope: sessionQueueKey})) and if getSummarySpendBackoffUntil(scope) is in the future, return {ok:true, compacted:false, reason:'summary spend backoff active'} without any LLM work.
3. Record spend for pending preparation: after each 'prepared' step, record the summarize call against the same scope with whatever accounting primitive the guards expose (mirror legacy compaction's bookkeeping — input size / output tokens if that is what it tracks). If the guard only tracks poor-reduction outcomes at publish time, record publish outcomes in the coordinator publish path instead and document why prepare steps are exempt.
4. Ensure drainPendingSummaryPreparationIfIdle treats the new reason as terminal for rescheduling (do not re-loop on 'summary spend backoff active' — check the reschedule condition at engine.ts ~714-720).

TESTS: engine-level test in test/index-runtime-llm-complete.test.ts or a focused engine test: with spend backoff active for the scope, a prepare drain performs zero summarize calls and returns the backoff reason; drain does not self-reschedule.

VERIFY: npx vitest run test/pending-summary-coordinator.test.ts + the engine test file touched
payload.title: Apply summary-spend guards to prepare-only drains

payload.description:
CONFIG FOOT-GUN / INFINITE LOOP: planPendingCondensedNodes (src/pending-summary-planner.ts ~202-227) normalizes condensedMinFanout with normalizePositiveInteger(value, 2), which accepts 1. With minFanout=1 (config allows it: src/db/config.ts ~687 only requires a number; toNumber does not floor to >=2) and any single node whose tokenCount >= condensedMinSourceTokens, planOneCondensedLayer creates a single-child parent at depth+1 on every loop iteration; the dedup filter only blocks same-depth+range collisions and depth increments each pass, so the while(true) loop in planPendingCondensedNodes never terminates — planner hangs the drain and allocates unboundedly.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. In planPendingCondensedNodes, clamp: const minFanout = Math.max(2, normalizePositiveInteger(input.condensedMinFanout, 2)).
2. Belt-and-braces: add a hard depth cap to the layering loop (e.g., stop when nextLayer depth exceeds 32 or after 32 iterations) so no future policy bug can hang the drain; log nothing, just stop planning.
3. Optionally clamp at config parse (src/db/config.ts) too, but the planner clamp is the load-bearing one since PendingCompactionCoordinatorConfig is also constructed directly in tests.

TESTS (test/pending-summary-planner.test.ts): condensedMinFanout=1 with a summarizable node terminates and produces no single-child parents; iteration cap holds under adversarial config (minFanout forced via direct planner input).

VERIFY: npx vitest run test/pending-summary-planner.test.ts
payload.title: Clamp condensedMinFanout >= 2 in pending condensed planner

payload.description:
CONSERVATIVE DEVIATION FROM AN 0002 (optional enhancement, lowest priority): AN 0002 says 'If the recalculated fresh tail shrinks the publishable interval, the frontier selection can choose a shorter contiguous prefix from the same prepared DAG.' The implementation instead marks the batch stale whenever batch.compactableEndOrdinal > snapshot.compactableEndOrdinal (getActiveBatchStaleReason, src/pending-summary-coordinator.ts ~536-553: 'pending batch range is no longer compactable'), discarding all prepared content. Correct but wasteful when the tail merely expanded to protect more recent messages.

IMPLEMENTATION SKETCH (careful — this reworks stale semantics):
1. In getActiveBatchStaleReason, when batch start >= current start AND batch end > current end, do not immediately stale: compute clampedEnd = snapshot.compactableEndOrdinal and run the remaining per-ordinal fingerprint + leaf sourceContextHash checks only over [batch.start, clampedEnd]. Leaf nodes straddling the clamp boundary (ordinalStart <= clampedEnd < ordinalEnd) are unusable; treat them as outside the publishable set, not as staleness.
2. In selectPublishFrontier, select over [batch.compactableStartOrdinal, min(batch.end, snapshot.compactableEndOrdinal)] instead of the raw batch range. Frontier nodes (esp. whole-prefix condensed) whose ordinalEnd exceeds the clamp cannot be used; selection must fall back to lower-depth nodes within the clamp (selectPendingPublishFrontier already does this naturally since it filters node.ordinalEnd <= endOrdinal).
3. Only stale the batch when NO contiguous frontier exists within the clamped range AND the per-ordinal checks fail.
4. Leave publish-time semantics unchanged otherwise: publisher still validates node readiness; publishIfReady still requires exact coverage of the (now clamped) range.

RISK NOTE: interacts with lc-395/lc-558 fixture changes; implement last. If the complexity/benefit tradeoff looks bad in practice (tail shrink is rare in append-mostly OpenClaw sessions), it is acceptable to close this as wontfix with a comment — but make that an explicit decision.

TESTS: coordinator test where freshTailCount growth clamps the publishable range -> publish succeeds over the shorter prefix using lower-depth nodes; whole-prefix condensed node correctly skipped; batch stales only when clamped range has no coverage.

VERIFY: npx vitest run test/pending-summary-coordinator.test.ts
payload.title: Trim publish frontier instead of staling when fresh tail shrinks
